title: Phase 1 QA pass: all six use cases end-to-end
description: Final integration gate (PRD §11 M6, §3): run UC1 (explain from session), UC2 (syntax question), UC3 (codebase tour), UC4 (interrogate + apply), UC5 (ask from app), UC6 (create project in-app) end-to-end on a clean environment. Verify the NFRs that are cheap to check: cold start <1s (N1), save→refresh <500ms and instant comments (N2), CLI <50ms warm (N3), artifact renders offline (N6). File issues for everything found; fix ship-blockers.
acceptance_criteria: All six use cases pass on a clean run-through; NFR spot-checks recorded; ship-blockers fixed, the rest filed.

title: Ask from Conceptify: composer + streaming progress (FR-5.1, FR-5.2)
description: Self-sufficient asking (PRD §7.5, UC5): 'New thread' in a project → question composer → spawns a headless agent (adapter + models.inAppAsk, cwd = project root) instructed to author per the artifact spec and publish via the CLI. While generating: streaming progress view parsing stream-json events (current activity, tool use) in the thread view (FR-2.3), swapping to the artifact viewer the moment save-artifact lands.
acceptance_criteria: Type a question in-app → watch live agent activity → artifact appears without touching a terminal; thread status transitions generating→ready; works in an auto-created (non-repo) topic project.

title: M5 checkpoint: UC4 end-to-end
description: Integration checkpoint for M5 (PRD §11): the full crown-jewel loop on a real artifact — comment on a diagram node AND a text selection AND a direct question → Ask follow-ups → three individual sidebar answers land live → Apply one clarification to the artifact → new version renders live, comment 'applied', other comments still anchored. Exercise cancel and a failure path too.
acceptance_criteria: UC4 demonstrated end-to-end on a real M3-generated artifact without manual intervention; cancel and failure paths behave per FR-4.8; rough edges filed as issues.

title: Skill follow-up guidance (FR-6.4)
description: Teach agents the follow-up contract (PRD FR-6.4): extend the skill files (the single home of authoring rules, reused by headless runs) with: how to consume get-context output; answering via resolve-comment (concise, sidebar-appropriate answers); apply mode — edit surgically or regenerate diagrams from embedded DSL sources, save a new version via save-artifact, and NEVER break existing data-cfy-ids (stability keeps other comments anchored, FR-4.4/FR-4.7).
acceptance_criteria: A headless run driven purely by the skill + assembled prompt correctly answers comments and performs an apply without manual prompt surgery; data-cfy-id stability rule stated explicitly and observed in a real apply run.

title: Run status UI: progress, cancel, failure surfacing (FR-4.8)
description: Visibility into in-flight runs (PRD FR-4.8): sidebar shows the active run — spinner, per-comment progress (which are answered so far), cancel button that kills the process. Failures surface the log tail inline; full runs/<id>.log retained and openable for debugging. Covers both answer-mode and apply-mode runs.
acceptance_criteria: During a batch run the UI shows live per-comment progress; cancel stops the run and marks it cancelled with partial answers preserved; a deliberately-failed run (bad binary path) shows the log tail and a path to the full log.

title: Apply to artifact: explicit second step (FR-4.7)
description: Live artifact evolution (PRD FR-4.7, UC4): per answered comment — or batch — 'Apply to artifact' spawns a run in apply mode (models.artifactUpdate): the agent edits/regenerates the relevant artifact portions (using embedded diagram DSL sources per FR-3.4 rather than hand-editing SVG; keeping existing data-cfy-ids stable), saves a NEW version via save-artifact, the viewer refreshes live, and the comments transition to 'applied'. Re-anchoring (M4) handles remaining comments against the new version. Simple Q&A never needs this mode — it exists for 'make this clearer' feedback.
acceptance_criteria: Apply on an answered comment produces artifact v(n+1), live viewer refresh, comment status 'applied'; other comments re-anchor or get 'reference moved'; diagram-targeted apply regenerates from embedded DSL source; thread status updating→ready throughout.

title: Ask follow-ups: batch sidebar-first flow (FR-4.6) + concurrency guard (FR-4.9)
description: The interrogation loop's answer half (PRD FR-4.6, UC4): 'Ask follow-ups' button gathers ALL open comments → spawns ONE headless run (adapter + models.followUp, cwd = project root) with a prompt assembled from get-context → the agent answers EACH comment individually via resolve-comment → sidebar updates live per-comment as answers land. NO artifact modification in this mode — answering and applying are deliberately separate steps. FR-4.9: enforce one follow-up run per thread at a time (button disabled + API guard while a run is active).
acceptance_criteria: With 3 open comments (mixed text/diagram/direct), one click produces 3 individual sidebar answers landing incrementally; artifact file untouched (same version); starting a second run on the same thread is rejected while the first is active; thread status correct throughout.

title: get-context + resolve-comment: API + CLI (the headless agent's I/O)
description: How headless agents read and write (PRD §5.2, §5.5): 'conceptify get-context --thread <id>' → thread + question + artifact path + open comments with anchors (JSON) — the run-specific context Conceptify assembles into the prompt; 'conceptify list-comments --thread <id> [--status open]'; 'conceptify resolve-comment --id <id> --answer-file <path> [--applied]' → marks answered/applied, stores answer_html, updates the sidebar live via comment-resolved event. These are the contract FR-6.4 teaches.
acceptance_criteria: get-context output contains everything an agent needs to answer comments without DB access; resolve-comment transitions status and the sidebar updates live; --applied variant sets applied status; answer file may be markdown or HTML fragment and renders correctly in the sidebar.

title: Headless agent spawner: process mgmt, streaming, timeout, cancel, logs
description: The engine that runs agents (PRD §5.1 agent spawner, FR-4.8/5.3 infrastructure): tokio::process::Command (NOT tauri-plugin-shell — frontend-initiated exec is disallowed, S3), kill_on_drop(true), children tracked in managed state, stdout/stderr streamed line-by-line to the frontend via Tauri events (stream-json parsed into activity events). Timeout (default 15 min, configurable) → kill + status error. Cancel = kill process group. Full transcript written to runs/<run-id>.log (§5.6) and FollowUpRun row maintained (§4). PATH problem: GUI apps get minimal PATH — resolve the agent binary via login-shell lookup (zsh -lc 'which claude') once and cache, or settings override (FR-7.3).
design: PRD §5.1, §4 FollowUpRun, N4. Status machine transitions (generating/updating/error) owned here + save-artifact.
acceptance_criteria: A run streams parsed progress events to the frontend; cancel kills the process tree promptly; timeout fires and marks the run failed; app quit reaps children (kill_on_drop); log file contains the full transcript; binary resolution works from a Finder-launched app; crashed run never corrupts thread state (N4).

title: Agent adapter + per-purpose model settings (§5.5)
description: The agent-agnostic invocation layer (PRD §5.5, G6, FR-7.1 backend): settings-defined command templates — adapters map name → {command, args with {prompt}/{model}/{project_root} placeholders, cwd}; defaultAdapter; per-purpose models {followUp, artifactUpdate, inAppAsk} so small sidebar answers don't burn a frontier model. Phase 1 ships and tests only the 'claude' adapter (claude -p ... --output-format stream-json --permission-mode acceptEdits) but ALL invocation flows go through the template — nothing hardcoded. Stored in SQLite settings; sensible defaults so zero config works when claude is on PATH.
design: PRD §5.5 JSON shape, §12 OQ3 (permission scoping investigated separately). Codex adapter itself is Phase 2 — this task just guarantees the seam.
acceptance_criteria: Adapter config round-trips through settings storage; template expansion covers prompt/model/cwd placeholders with correct shell-safe arg handling (args array, no string splitting); swapping the model per purpose changes the spawned command; a hypothetical second adapter can be added purely via config.

title: Anchor re-attachment across versions + 'reference moved' (FR-4.4)
description: Anchors must survive artifact updates (PRD FR-4.4): on switching or receiving a new artifact version, re-attach each comment — primary strategy: same data-cfy-id + offsets; fallback: text-quote search with prefix/suffix. If re-anchoring fails, flag the comment 'reference moved' in the sidebar — NEVER silently drop it. This is what makes the M5 apply-to-artifact loop safe: applying a clarification can't orphan the other comments invisibly.
design: PRD FR-4.4, FR-6.4 (skill instructs agents to keep data-cfy-ids stable precisely so this succeeds).
acceptance_criteria: Test matrix: (a) unchanged element → anchor holds; (b) element text edited but data-cfy-id kept → re-anchors via id or quote; (c) element removed → 'reference moved' badge, comment still readable and answerable; verified against real v1→v2 artifact pairs.

title: Comments sidebar: filters, statuses, scroll-to-anchor, highlights (FR-4.5)
description: The interrogation home base (PRD FR-4.5): sidebar listing all comments for the thread, filterable open/answered/applied, each showing anchor excerpt, body, and — once resolved — the agent's answer rendered from HTML/markdown. Clicking a comment scrolls the artifact to its anchor (via bridge); open comments show subtle persistent highlights in the artifact. Live-updates from comment events (the M5 run flow will land answers here one by one).
acceptance_criteria: Filters work; clicking a comment scrolls the artifact to the anchor and flashes it; answer HTML renders safely in the sidebar; a comment created/resolved via API appears/updates live without refresh.

title: Direct follow-up composer (FR-4.3)
description: Free-text questions without a selection (PRD FR-4.3, §4): a composer at the bottom of the thread view creating a Comment with null anchor — flows through the identical sidebar/resolution machinery as anchored comments. This is deliberately NOT a chat box: it queues an open comment for the next follow-up run.
acceptance_criteria: Typed question appears in the sidebar as an open comment with no anchor excerpt; submits on Cmd+Enter; empty submissions rejected.

title: Diagram-element comments via data-cfy-id (FR-4.2)
description: Comment on diagrams, not just prose (PRD FR-4.2, UC4): any data-cfy-id-bearing element (SVG nodes, edges, groups; section headings) gets a hover affordance; clicking opens the comment popover anchored to that element id. This is why FR-3.3 mandates data-cfy-id coverage — verify the pipeline works on real M3-generated artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Hovering a diagram node shows the affordance; click → popover → save creates an element-anchored comment; the element gets a persistent highlight; works on a real D2/DOT-generated inline SVG from an M3 artifact.

title: Text-selection comments: popover + anchor capture (FR-4.1)
description: The first interrogation gesture (PRD FR-4.1, UC4): select text in the artifact → 'Add comment' popover appears near the selection → typing + saving creates a comment with full anchor data (data-cfy-id + offsets primary, text-quote+prefix/suffix fallback) captured via the bridge. Saved comment immediately shows a subtle persistent highlight in the artifact and appears in the sidebar.
acceptance_criteria: Select → popover → save → highlight + sidebar entry, all feeling instant (<100ms perceived, N2); anchors captured correctly for selections spanning inline elements; popover dismisses cleanly on click-away/Escape.

title: Comments backend: CRUD API + anchor model (FR-4.4)
description: Comment storage and API (PRD §7.4, §4): create/list/update comments with the FR-4.4 anchoring model stored as JSON — primary anchor = data-cfy-id + character offsets within that element; fallback = text-quote with prefix/suffix (W3C Web Annotation style) for re-attachment after artifact updates. Statuses open | answered | applied; answer_html field for agent resolutions; null anchor = direct follow-up question (§4). Mutations emit Tauri events for live sidebar updates. Endpoints under /api/v1/ (list-comments with --status filter serves both the UI and the M5 CLI).
design: PRD FR-4.4, §4 Comment entity. Anchor JSON schema documented in the API reference doc — the bridge, re-anchoring, and headless agents all consume it.
acceptance_criteria: Comments persist with both primary and fallback anchor data; null-anchor comments accepted; status transitions enforced (open→answered→applied); list filters by status; comment mutations emit events.
notes:
IMPLEMENTATION (94m.2 comments backend — CRUD + anchor model). Left in_progress, NOT closed/committed per orchestrator.

ANCHOR JSON SCHEMA (FR-4.4) — canonical def in crates/conceptify-types (Anchor/TextAnchor/ElementAnchor/TextQuote), documented in docs/api.md 'Comments > anchor model'. snake_case throughout (rest of the API is snake_case; the JS bridge must emit snake_case too). Envelope: every anchor is a JSON object with integer 'v' (currently 1) and a 'type' discriminator.
  - type='text' (FR-4.1 selection): primary = cfy_id (nearest ancestor data-cfy-id) + start/end char offsets within that element's normalized textContent; fallback = quote{exact, prefix?, suffix?} (W3C text-quote). cfy_id/start/end are OPTIONAL — omitted when selection has no id-bearing ancestor (quote is then sole anchor). quote REQUIRED.
  - type='element' (FR-4.2 diagram/heading click): primary = cfy_id (REQUIRED); quote OPTIONAL fallback (omit for graphical nodes w/o text).
  - null anchor (field absent) = direct follow-up question (FR-4.3).
  Server validates the ENVELOPE only (object, v==1, known type, required fields present) then stores the object VERBATIM (compact JSON) — unknown extra fields are TOLERATED and survive round-trip, so 94m.1/94m.3-4 bridge can add capture hints (e.g. captured_rect) without a server change. Unsupported v / unknown type / not-object / missing required field -> 400 InvalidAnchor.

ANCHOR_STATE: added column via APPENDED migration #8 COMMENT_ANCHOR_STATE ('ALTER TABLE comments ADD COLUMN anchor_state TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''anchored'' CHECK (anchor_state IN (''anchored'',''moved''))'). Chose a first-class column, NOT a field inside the anchor blob: the anchor JSON is authoring-time truth (immutable, opaque, stored verbatim); whether it still resolves is mutable runtime state, so a plain UPDATE avoids read-parse-rewrite and the sidebar can badge off a queryable field. 94m.7 OWNS the POLICY of when to flip anchored->moved; this bead only stores/serves it + provides the write path (PATCH anchor_state). null-anchor comments stay 'anchored' forever.

STATUS MACHINE: open|answered|applied, MONOTONIC — status may only advance or stay (rank open<answered<applied), regression rejected 409 with structured {error, from, to}. open->applied DIRECTLY ALLOWED (decided per FR-4.7 + M5 resolve-comment --applied one-shot: apply-mode can resolve-with-update a never-answered comment). resolved_at stamped (DB clock, in SQL) the FIRST time a comment leaves open, stable thereafter.

ENDPOINTS (flat, mirroring threads):
  - POST /api/v1/comments  body {thread_id, artifact_version, anchor:JSON|null, body}. Requires thread + (thread,artifact_version) to exist (explicit checks -> clean 404 instead of opaque composite-FK error). Empty/whitespace body -> 400. -> 200 CommentResponse.
  - GET  /api/v1/comments?thread_id=<id>[&status=open|answered|applied]  oldest-first. Unknown thread -> empty array (no 404, matches list_threads). Invalid status -> 400. Serves UI + M5 list-comments.
  - PATCH /api/v1/comments/{id}  body any subset of {status, answer_html, anchor_state}; >=1 required else 400. Drives M5 resolve-comment. -> 200 full updated CommentResponse.
  NO delete (PRD doesn't need it Phase 1).

EVENTS: comment-created {project_id, thread_id, comment_id} on POST; comment-updated {project_id, thread_id, comment_id, status} on PATCH. DECISION: named it 'comment-updated' (not the PRD's sketch 'comment-resolved') — one event covers ALL comment mutations (status, answer_html landing, anchor_state 'reference moved' flip), consistent with artifact-updated/thread-created. b12.3 (resolve-comment) MUST listen for 'comment-updated', NOT 'comment-resolved'. Documented in docs/api.md Events table. Payload carries project_id+thread_id so frontend scopes refetch.

FK NOTE for 94m.1/94m.3-5: a comment can only be created against an artifact version that already exists (composite FK comments(thread_id,artifact_version)->artifacts(thread_id,version)). So the bridge must send the CURRENT rendered artifact_version. A thread still 'generating' with no artifact -> ArtifactVersionNotFound 404.

FILES: crates/conceptify-types/src/lib.rs (Anchor/TextAnchor/ElementAnchor/TextQuote + CreateCommentRequest/CommentResponse/ListCommentsResponse/UpdateCommentRequest; anchor carried as serde_json::Value opaque); src-tauri/src/comments.rs (new domain module, 19 unit tests); src-tauri/src/server/comments_routes.rs (new, 4 MockRuntime HTTP-harness tests incl listen_any event capture); src-tauri/src/db/migrations.rs (COMMENT_ANCHOR_STATE append); server/mod.rs + routes.rs (wiring); lib.rs (mod comments + comments_crud_against_real_migrations integration test proving anchor_state column + CHECK + composite FK live); docs/api.md (Comments section: anchor schema, status machine, 3 endpoints, Events table). GATES: just check clean (clippy -D warnings), cargo test --workspace 98+10 pass. New files rustfmt-clean.

DID NOT TOUCH: frontend src/**, CLI, skill/**, justfile, docs/cli.md, docs/artifact-spec.md. Did NOT implement re-attachment (94m.7).

title: Bridge script + injection: the in-artifact interaction layer
description: The postMessage bridge (PRD §5.4): replace the M2 no-op stub with the real injected script providing (a) text-selection reporting — ranges/quotes to the shell for the comment popover; (b) click-to-comment on any data-cfy-id-bearing element with hover affordance; (c) rendering highlight decorations for existing comment anchors; (d) scroll-to-anchor on request from the sidebar. Shell side: validate event.source === iframe.contentWindow on every message (opaque-origin frames report origin 'null'); define a versioned message protocol. Injection stays server-side in the protocol handler so on-disk files remain pristine.
design: PRD §5.4 bridge protocol, §9 S2. Keep the protocol small and versioned — M4/M5 features all ride on it.
acceptance_criteria: From inside a real artifact: selecting text reports quote+range to the shell; clicking a data-cfy-id element reports its id; the shell can command a highlight decoration and a smooth scroll-to-anchor; messages from any other source are ignored; on-disk artifact unchanged.
notes:
Bridge implemented (94m.1). Decisions for downstream beads (94m.3/4/5/6/7):

PROTOCOL (docs/api.md 'Bridge protocol' is the contract). Envelope {cfy:1, type,...}. Artifact->shell: ready | selection {anchor,rect} | selection_cleared | element_click {anchor,rect}. Shell->artifact: set_highlights {highlights:[{key,anchor}]} (FULL replacement; [] clears) | scroll_to_anchor {anchor, key?}. Unknown types dropped both sides (shell logs at debug).

OFFSET CONVENTION (94m.7 must match): text-anchor start/end = UTF-16 code-unit offsets into the element's VISIBLE TEXT = concatenation of Text node data in doc order EXCLUDING script/style/noscript/template subtrees, NO whitespace normalization. This is the precise meaning of api.md's 'normalized text content' (clarifying pointer added there). Rationale: offsets stay stable against inline-JS/CSS churn and against the injected bridge itself (verified in harness: suffix context skips artifact <script> text).

QUOTES: text-anchor quotes are raw visible-text slices; prefix/suffix = up to 32 chars of DOCUMENT-wide visible text (omitted when empty). Element-anchor quotes are whitespace-collapsed+trimmed element text, omitted if empty or >300 chars (re-attachment hint only, not exact-searchable).

RECTS (94m.3): {x,y,width,height} in IFRAME-VIEWPORT CSS px (getBoundingClientRect at send time). Popover position = iframe element's own bounding rect + msg rect. Rects go stale on artifact scroll -> dismiss/re-request on scroll or selection_cleared.

HOVER AFFORDANCE DECISION: always-on subtle dashed outline on [data-cfy-id] hover; NO commenting-enabled toggle message in v1 (add a set_mode message later if ever needed). All injected style is :where()-wrapped specificity-0 (incl. :hover INSIDE :where — outside it out-ranks the highlight rules; found+fixed in harness).

LIFECYCLE (94m.6): 'ready' fires after EVERY document load incl. version-switch reloads, which wipe decorations -> consumers must re-send set_highlights on every ready. bridge.ts queues commands sent before the first ready of an attachment. attach() is idempotent per element; ThreadView registers via a stable useCallback ref.

ELEMENT_CLICK suppression: clicks that end a non-collapsed selection, and clicks on a[href]/button/form controls/summary/label/[contenteditable], do not fire.

DECORATIONS: element -> data-cfy-hl='element' attr (outline); text -> inline span[data-cfy-hl='text'] wraps via splitText, fully unwrapped + Node.normalize() on clear (harness-verified textContent byte-identical after clear). Text resolution: offsets (verified against quote) -> doc-wide quote search (prefix/suffix scored) -> outline cfy_id host as coarse fallback; unresolvable skipped ('moved' flagging is 94m.7's job). SVG text nodes are never wrapped (namespace check) — falls back to element outline.

TRUST: artifact JS CAN spoof artifact->shell messages (accepted, S2 containment; shell shape-validates everything); it CANNOT spoof shell->artifact commands (event.source !== window.parent; harness-verified). Both directions post with targetOrigin '*' (opaque origin unnameable); nothing sensitive rides the channel.

ASSET: script lives at src-tauri/assets/bridge.js, inlined via concat!(include_str!) into artifact_protocol::BRIDGE_TAG (marker data-cfy-bridge="v1"); a test guards that the JS contains no literal script-tag open/close and references all six protocol message names.

title: M3 checkpoint: quality iteration on ≥3 real repos (UC1/UC2/UC3)
description: Integration checkpoint for M3 (PRD §11): iterate the skill on real questions across at least 3 real repos until artifact quality is consistently good — exercising UC1 (explain a subsystem from a session), UC2 (unfamiliar-syntax micro-tutorial), UC3 (whole-codebase orientation tour). Each iteration feeds fixes back into the skill authoring guide / scaffold / spec. Done means: start-to-finish from a COLD app, artifacts consistently beautiful and correct.
acceptance_criteria: UC1, UC2, UC3 each demonstrated end-to-end from a cold app on real repos; recurring quality failures have corresponding skill-guide fixes; remaining rough edges filed as issues.

title: Author Claude Code skill v1: flow + authoring guide + visual strategy
description: The product's other half (PRD §7.6): the skill at ~/.claude/skills/conceptify/ (source lives in this repo; installed globally). FR-6.1 trigger & flow: on 'use conceptify to explain X' — check CLI availability → ensure-project --dir <repo root> → create-thread → author artifact → save-artifact (app launch is free via the CLI contract). FR-6.2 authoring guide: embed the artifact spec (§7.3), the design-system scaffold template + component classes (from M2), explanation-quality guidance (hook → mental model → visuals → walkthrough → summary; visuals wherever they beat prose), and the §8 tiered visual strategy with EXACT generation-time render commands (d2 --layout=elk, dot -Tsvg, Shiki via node). Must instruct: never commit .conceptify/, suggest global gitignore.
design: PRD §7.6, §8, §5.5 surface 1. Authoring rules live in exactly one place — the skill files — reused verbatim by headless runs in M5.
acceptance_criteria: In a real repo with the skill installed, 'use conceptify to explain <topic>' produces a spec-compliant artifact visible in the app with zero manual steps; the skill file embeds (not paraphrases) the current scaffold + spec; render commands verified to work as written.
notes:
Skill v1 decisions (57l.1):
1. LAYOUT: skill/ is the source of truth — SKILL.md (flow FR-6.1 + authoring core FR-6.2 + WKWebView constraints), references/rendering.md (tiered visual strategy + verified render commands + SVG post-processing), scripts/highlight.mjs (Shiki helper), plus the existing design-system.css/.md and examples/demo-artifact.html. 'just install-skill' snapshots skill/ AND docs/artifact-spec.md into ~/.claude/skills/conceptify/ (copy, not symlink — install is an explicit snapshot step; repo files stay canonical, nothing hand-duplicated into prose).
2. D5 REFINEMENT (supersedes the sentinel-hex idea for d2/dot): d2 0.7.1 emits stable theme classes (fill-B6, stroke-B1, .connection) and graphviz 15.1.0 emits class=node/edge/cluster; artifact CSS class rules beat SVG presentation attributes, so theming is done by pasting per-tool 'adapter CSS' into the artifact's second <style> block — no hex rewriting. Post-processing on the SVG itself: strip prolog + d2's embedded <style> blocks (base64 fonts + baked palette, ~15KB/diagram), strip graphviz positional id= attrs, stamp data-cfy-id (d2: base64-decode the <g> class = HTML-escaped DSL key, e.g. 'KGNsaWVudCAtJmd0OyBtaWRkbGV3YXJlKVswXQ==' -> '(client -> middleware)[0]'; dot: child <title>). All verified empirically.
3. SHIKI: shiki has no CLI and npx can't resolve ESM libs, so the skill ships scripts/highlight.mjs which bootstraps shiki@^4 into ~/.cache/conceptify/shiki-env (npm --prefix) and imports it via a shim module written into that dir (ESM resolution is relative to the importing file). vitesse-light/vitesse-dark dual themes, --highlight line ranges via transformer. Survives skill reinstall (cache lives outside ~/.claude/skills).
4. TOOLCHAIN: d2 0.7.1 + graphviz 15.1.0 installed via brew on this machine (were missing); node 22 present. All documented commands run as written: 'd2 --layout=elk --pad 0', 'dot -Tsvg', highlight.mjs.
5. E2E VERIFIED (isolated HOME, app built from HEAD ca59e50 in a temp worktree): ensure-project on this repo -> create-thread -> authored a genuine artifact (launch-and-wait contract; d2 diagram with 14 stamped anchors + 2 Shiki listings + full component usage) following SKILL.md verbatim -> save-artifact returned {version:1, warningsCount:0}, thread status ready, artifact.v1.html + artifact.html in central storage. Rendering visually verified in Chrome light AND dark (agent-browser screenshots); in-app pixel check blocked by missing screen-recording permission, but the viewer serves the same validated bytes and focus-after-save succeeded.
6. AUTHORING LESSON folded into rendering.md: long direction:right d2 chains render small at column width — prefer direction:down for 6+ node flows.
7. SLOTS for later beads: SKILL.md step 5 is the self-review insertion point (57l.2); scope note reserves references/follow-ups.md for FR-6.4 (b12.7); doctor (57l.3) referenced in rendering.md tool-availability section.

title: M2 checkpoint: hand-written test artifact renders beautifully everywhere
description: Integration checkpoint for M2 (PRD §11): hand-write a rich test artifact (exercising the full design system, inline SVG diagrams with data-cfy-id, Shiki-rendered code, metadata block — a real explanation, not lorem ipsum) and verify it renders beautifully in-app through the full pipeline (CLI save-artifact → artifact:// → sandboxed iframe) AND standalone in Safari and Chrome. Fix whatever this shakes out; keep the artifact in-repo as a golden test fixture for viewer/validator regressions.
acceptance_criteria: Same .html file: gorgeous in-app, in Safari, and in Chrome; light and dark; offline; checked into the repo as a fixture; any pipeline bugs found are fixed or filed.
notes:
M2 checkpoint verification pass (nsy.7). Ran the app from a HEAD (cf93696) worktree via 'npm run tauri dev' under an isolated HOME (real DB untouched, mtime unchanged; CARGO_HOME/RUSTUP_HOME pinned to the real home so the toolchain resolved). Drove the REAL CLI against it: status -> ensure-project -> create-thread -> save-artifact skill/examples/demo-artifact.html => version 1, warningsCount 0, thread status 'ready', window auto-focused. Second save => version 2 (follow_up), v1 file immutable (sha unchanged), and the CLI surfaced W-VERSION-MISMATCH (warningsCount 1) — validator warning path confirmed end-to-end. Byte-identity: stored artifact.v1.html == artifact.html == input (sha256 43728510...), so the in-app iframe serves exactly the file I verified in browsers.

VISUAL RESULTS (gorgeous in every case: editorial palette, serif display, dual-theme Shiki code swaps correctly, SVG diagrams themed, callouts/steps/table/details all correct):
- Chrome (agent-browser, real Blink): light + dark + offline, full-page. HAR shows ONLY the document load — zero external requests; explicit 'set offline on' reload renders identically. Artifact audited: no <script>/<link>/remote fonts; the only external refs are text inside a Shiki code block and one click-only <a href> to MDN.
- Safari / in-app WKWebView (real WebKit, offscreen WKWebView.takeSnapshot — same engine as the app iframe AND Safari): light + dark. In-app proxy rendered the EXACT served bytes (file + injected bridge stub) — stub doesn't affect layout. Widths 288/640/900.

CAPTURED vs PROXIED: screencapture is blocked (screen-recording permission denied: 'could not create image from display'), so the actual app window and Safari window could not be pixel-captured. In-app pixels are PROXIED via a real WKWebView snapshot of the served bytes (identical engine + identical bytes); the pipeline itself was verified FUNCTIONALLY (save->ready->focus, deterministic iframe src artifact://localhost/<thread>/<n>, protocol handler unit-tested). Safari.app opened the file (renders fine) but AppleScript control hangs on the TCC automation prompt.

BUG FOUND -> filed conceptify-nsy.8 (P2): inline <code> overflows horizontally below ~458px pane width (overflow-wrap:normal on a 42-char unbreakable token). Not fixed here: design-system.css is a closed nsy.5 deliverable + a parallel agent was active, and acceptance permits filing; the demo embeds the scaffold verbatim so both must change together. Everything else (pre/tables/SVGs) scales/clips correctly; at >=460px pane the artifact is flawless.

FIXTURE DECISION: keep the golden fixture at skill/examples/demo-artifact.html (single source of truth; already referenced by 'just install-skill' and the skill authoring guide). Do NOT duplicate into src-tauri/tests/fixtures/ — a Rust regression test should reference it via the workspace path (CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR/../../skill/examples/demo-artifact.html). Duplicating would drift from the skill's canonical copy.

title: CLI: save-artifact command
description: 'conceptify save-artifact --thread <id> --file <path>' → {version:n} (PRD §5.2): uploads the file to the save-artifact endpoint, prints validation warnings to stderr (agent-visible) and the version JSON to stdout, and relies on the endpoint's side effects (live refresh + focus of the thread). This is the skill's publish step — the last command in the UC1 flow.
acceptance_criteria: Saving from the CLI live-refreshes an open viewer and focuses the app on the thread; validation warnings appear on stderr; allowlist violations exit non-zero with the reason.

title: Design-system CSS scaffold + type/visual design pass (D1-D6)
description: What makes artifacts a delight to read (PRD §6, goal G2). Build the CSS scaffold that (a) gets embedded into every artifact by the skill and (b) shares tokens with the app shell. D1 typography: pick final fonts (serif headings — Newsreader / Fraunces / Source Serif 4 candidates; Inter or system-ui body; JetBrains Mono / SF Mono code) with robust system-stack fallbacks so artifacts never break offline — this resolves OQ1. D2 tokens: type scale, spacing rhythm, ~68ch measure, full light/dark palette as CSS variables via prefers-color-scheme. D3 content components: callouts (insight/warning/definition), step sequences, key-term highlights, collapsible deep-dives (<details>), figure+caption, comparison tables. D4 code blocks: styles for pre-rendered Shiki v4 dual-theme output. D5 diagram styling hooks: same CSS variables consumed by D2/Mermaid themeVariables/hand-authored SVG (currentColor + var()); optional hand-drawn flavor. D6 motion: CSS-first, compositor-friendly (transform/opacity only — WKWebView 60fps cap), fully readable with JS disabled.
design: PRD §6, §12 OQ1. The scaffold matters more than any diagram library choice — budget real design iteration time here.
acceptance_criteria: A demo artifact using every D3 component + a Shiki code block + a styled SVG renders beautifully in light AND dark, in WKWebView AND Safari/Chrome, offline, with JS disabled; scaffold lives in-repo as a template the skill embeds; OQ1 font decision recorded.
notes:
OQ1 RESOLVED (D1 final typography): headings = New York via ui-serif with Iowan Old Style as the deterministic named fallback for Chromium-on-macOS (then Palatino/Georgia); body = system sans stack (SF Pro on macOS) — Inter rejected because web fonts are banned (N6, artifact-spec §1) and Inter is not preinstalled; code = SF Mono via ui-monospace with Menlo fallback — JetBrains Mono deliberately NOT listed so FR-6.3 self-review screenshots match what readers see (determinism over preference); hand-drawn flavor token --cfy-font-hand (Bradley Hand/Chalkboard SE). Visual language: 'quiet print editorial' — warm paper #fbf9f4 / warm charcoal #17140f, single terracotta accent (#a34d24 light / #e09863 dark), hairline rules, tracked small-caps labels, serif display with negative tracking. Full rationale in skill/design-system.md.

Deliverables: skill/design-system.css (embeddable scaffold, tokens fenced by /* @cfy:tokens:start|end */ sentinels for later app-shell import), skill/design-system.md (OQ1 record + component vocabulary + Mermaid/d2 token mapping), skill/examples/demo-artifact.html (exemplary artifact: every D3 component, hand-written Shiki v4 vitesse-light/dark dual-theme block, two SVG diagrams — one with cfy:src d2 comment, one bespoke hand-drawn — 23 data-cfy-ids; validates against artifact-spec §8 with 0 errors 0 warnings via a file-only validator implementing the rule set). Shiki theme pair decision: vitesse-light/vitesse-dark.

CRITICAL D6 FINDING (verified empirically with a headless WKWebView snapshot tool): occluded/off-screen WKWebViews SUSPEND CSS animations indefinitely — an opacity-0 fade-in with fill-mode:both stayed invisible forever in snapshots (exactly the FR-6.3 headless-review scenario). Scaffold rule adopted: no animation may hide content in its from-state; .cfy-reveal is transform-only (translateY), and there is deliberately no stroke draw-in class. The M3 skill (57l.1) must teach this rule for artifact-specific styles too.

Visual verification: full-page + per-component screenshots in headless Chromium (agent-browser, light+dark, prefers-color-scheme emulated) AND genuine WKWebView (custom swift snapshot tool, light+dark via NSAppearance) — all reviewed frame by frame. JS-disabled readability is structural: the demo contains zero script tags.

title: Artifact viewer: sandboxed iframe + version switcher + open-in-browser
description: The reading surface (PRD §5.4, FR-2.3/2.4/2.5): thread view embeds <iframe src="artifact://localhost/<thread>/<version>" sandbox="allow-scripts"> — NO allow-same-origin, so the artifact runs in an opaque origin and cannot touch app DOM, Tauri IPC, storage, or the API. App-shell CSP in tauri.conf.json stays strict, allowing frame-src artifact:. Subscribe to artifact-updated for live refresh. Version history switcher (FR-2.4; visual diff is P2). 'Open in browser' button opens artifact.html with the system default browser — the permanently-one-click portability guarantee (FR-2.5).
design: PRD §5.4, §9 S2: capabilities scoped to the main window, withGlobalTauri off.
acceptance_criteria: Artifact renders in the thread view; saving a new version refreshes the iframe in place in <500ms; switcher flips between versions; open-in-browser opens the same content in the default browser; devtools confirm the iframe origin is opaque and Tauri IPC is unreachable from it.
notes:
Viewer implementation decisions (nsy.4):
- Iframe sandbox is exactly sandbox="allow-scripts" (no allow-same-origin) — S2 boundary. Verified live: contentDocument=null and contentWindow property access throws from the parent; artifact side has no IPC path.
- The viewer loads CONCRETE version URLs (artifact://localhost/<thread>/<N>) even when the switcher is on 'Latest': appStore tracks the version list and 'latest' resolves to max known version client-side. Rationale: numeric URLs are immutable+cacheable (protocol sends immutable cache headers), and the artifact-updated payload carries the concrete version, so live refresh is a synchronous src flip (measured 11–46ms event→iframe load, well under N2's 500ms) with no round-trip on the critical path. artifact://.../latest is only a fallback while the version list is unknown.
- handleArtifactUpdated (appStore) optimistically appends the event's version to artifactVersions, then refetches for truth; also refetches projects + threads (status flip to ready, ordering). All artifact-updated wiring lives in src/lib/events.ts -> appStore seam, per the qxr.5 pattern.
- Version switcher state (viewerVersion: number|'latest') lives in appStore, reset on thread/project change. Pinned old versions show a read-only badge and do NOT follow new saves; 'Latest' does (FR-2.4).
- New tauri commands: list_artifact_versions (ascending; unknown thread => empty list) and open_artifact_in_browser (resolves ~/Documents/.../artifact.html server-side via new artifacts::latest_copy_path helper, opens via tauri_plugin_opener::open_path; returns the path). Frontend never builds filesystem paths (FR-2.5).
- App-shell CSP set in tauri.conf.json: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; font-src 'self' data:; connect-src ipc: http://ipc.localhost; frame-src artifact:; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'none'; form-action 'none'. devCsp additionally allows ws://localhost:1420 http://localhost:1420 for Vite HMR.
- CSP enforcement facts (verified empirically on the built app, tauri build --debug): Tauri v2 delivers the CSP via response header on the tauri:// protocol (no meta tag in DOM); eval() is blocked (script-src enforced, EvalError) and an external img fires securitypolicyviolation (img-src enforced) while Tauri IPC keeps working. In DEV (remote devUrl -> Vite), Tauri cannot apply a CSP to the page at all — devCsp is effectively inert with a devUrl; the production CSP is the load-bearing one. Do not assume dev mode tests the CSP.
- Generating/updating with no versions => pulsing 'Generating artifact…' placeholder (keeps the question card); status error with no versions => failure state (logs are M5); unknown/edge versions inside the iframe are handled by the protocol's styled error pages.

title: save-artifact endpoint: validation, versioning, storage layout, live refresh
description: The ingestion path (FR-3.6, §5.6): POST save-artifact stores an HTML file for a thread as a new version (v1, v2, ...; prior versions retained; created_by initial|follow_up), using the §5.6 layout — threads/<slug>/artifact.vN.html + artifact.html copy/symlink of latest + runs/ dir (final root per decision OQ2). Writes are atomic (temp + rename, N4). Validation per the artifact spec: parseable HTML, size sanity warn > ~5MB, no <script src> outside the Tier-2 allowlist (hard fail), data-cfy-id presence (warn when coverage thin); warnings surface to the caller as structured JSON, not hard failures. On success: thread status → ready, artifact-updated Tauri event → viewer live-refresh (< 500ms, N2).
design: PRD §7.3 FR-3.6, §5.6, N2/N4. Blocked on decision OQ2 for the storage root.
acceptance_criteria: Saving twice yields v1 and v2 with both files on disk and latest pointer updated; a disallowed <script src> is rejected; thin data-cfy-id coverage and >5MB produce warnings in the JSON response; kill -9 during save never leaves a corrupt/partial visible artifact; artifact-updated event fires with thread + version.
notes:
Implemented. Key decisions:

1. REQUEST SHAPE: raw HTML bytes (POST /api/v1/threads/{id}/artifact, Content-Type: text/html, not enforced) — not JSON/multipart. A JSON wrapper cannot carry invalid UTF-8, making the spec's E-UTF8 rule unreachable, and escapes multi-MB payloads; raw bytes let the validator own E-UTF8/E-SIZE-MAX verbatim and the CLI just streams the file. DefaultBodyLimit=60MiB on this route so 50–60MiB files get the structured E-SIZE-MAX instead of a bare 413.

2. created_by INFERRED: version 1 = initial, >=2 = follow_up. Never caller-supplied.

3. VALIDATOR: scraper (html5ever) with manual tree traversal (no CSS selectors — avoids SVG namespace pitfalls for shape counting / data-cfy-id checks). All spec §8 rules implemented verbatim incl. the §7.1 allowlist match rule (prefix + next-char ./ + charset + no .. segment, case-sensitive), @import scanning in inline CSS (code position → E-EXTERNAL-CODE; relative @import → W-LOCAL-REF), cfy:src comment adjacency (W-SRC-ORPHAN), W-VERSION-MISMATCH against the server-assigned version. Hand-rolled id-grammar/kv/css scanners — no regex crate.

4. ATOMICITY/ORDERING (N4): whole pipeline (version query → validate → temp+rename file writes → single DB tx inserting artifact row + thread→ready) runs inside the one shared connection lock (db::with_conn_result closure), so concurrent saves serialize — no version races. Files written BEFORE the DB row: a crash between leaves an orphan file no row references (invisible; overwritten on retry since version derives from the DB), never a row pointing at a missing file. artifact.html is an atomic COPY of the latest version, temp+rename, never a symlink.

5. ApiState MADE GENERIC over tauri::Runtime (default Wry — zero production change) so HTTP-level tests drive the REAL router (auth middleware + routing + emit) against tauri::test MockRuntime. Future route beads (comments, get-context) inherit this harness (see server/artifacts_routes.rs tests::Harness). artifacts_root() has a cfg(test)-only CONCEPTIFY_TEST_ARTIFACTS_DIR env override (process-wide → set once via OnceLock in tests).

6. Error response: 422 with { error, code } (spec shape, first violation) plus errors[] listing all hard failures. Event artifact-updated payload: { project_id, thread_id, version } (docs/api.md Events table).

7. No migration needed — the M0 artifacts table already had version/file_path/created_by/UNIQUE(thread_id,version).

For nsy.6 (CLI): read file as bytes, POST raw body, print warnings[] as 'warning: CODE: message' to stderr, exit non-zero on non-2xx using code/error fields. For nsy.4 (viewer): listen artifact-updated, reload iframe for thread_id/version. For nsy.2: files live at ~/Documents/conceptify/artifacts/<project-id>/threads/<slug>/artifact.vN.html; artifacts.file_path DB column holds the absolute vN path.

title: artifact:// protocol handler + per-response CSP
description: Serve artifacts through a custom URI scheme (PRD §5.4): register_asynchronous_uri_scheme_protocol for artifact://localhost/<thread>/<version> — handler reads the HTML from disk, injects the bridge script (injection point built now; the bridge itself is M4 — inject a no-op stub until then), and responds with a restrictive per-response CSP: allow inline script/style, connect-src 'none' (artifact JS can never reach the localhost API), allow only the §8 Tier-2 pinned CDN hosts. Server-side injection keeps the .html file on disk pristine and portable — no Conceptify residue (G3).
design: PRD §5.4, §9 S2, Appendix A. Cross-scheme = real origin isolation. Note wry #168: inline SVG works, via custom protocol has gotchas.
acceptance_criteria: artifact:// URL renders a test HTML file; response carries the CSP header; a fetch() from artifact JS to 127.0.0.1:4477 is blocked; the on-disk file is byte-identical after serving; unknown thread/version returns a clean error page.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED (src-tauri/src/artifact_protocol.rs; wiring in lib.rs; version_file_path helper appended to artifacts.rs; docs/api.md gained 'The artifact:// scheme' section — the URL contract nsy.4 consumes).

URL CONTRACT: artifact://localhost/<thread-id>/<version>. <version> = bare decimal integer >= 1 (serves immutable artifact.vN.html, Cache-Control: public,max-age=31536000,immutable) OR literal lowercase 'latest' (resolved via DB MAX(version), serves that versioned file, no-store). DECISION: latest is DB-resolved, NOT the artifact.html disk copy — files are written before the DB row commits (N4 crash ordering), so the copy can be ahead of the DB; DB is the source of truth the version switcher/events read. GET only (405 otherwise). No percent-decoding anywhere: segments validated against [A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,128} / digits, so %, ., backslash, empty segments all 400 — traversal structurally impossible; DB-returned project_id/slug re-validated as single path segments (defense in depth vs corrupted DB).

CSP (every response, success + error pages, verbatim spec section-3 reference policy): default-src 'none'; script-src 'unsafe-inline' https://cdn.jsdelivr.net; style-src 'unsafe-inline' https://cdn.jsdelivr.net; font-src data: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net; img-src data:; connect-src 'none'. Plus text/html;charset=utf-8 and nosniff. Keep in lockstep with spec 7.1 allowlist + artifacts::CDN_ALLOWLIST.

BRIDGE INJECTION (for 94m.1): single seam = artifact_protocol::inject_bridge + BRIDGE_STUB const. Stub is <script data-cfy-bridge="stub"> defining window.__cfyBridge = Object.freeze({stub:true}); spliced before the LAST case-insensitive </body> (last occurrence skips </body> literals in script text; whole DOM parsed when it runs — no DOMContentLoaded needed), appended at EOF if no close tag, idempotent via the reserved data-cfy-bridge marker. M4 replaces BRIDGE_STUB content only; injection point/mechanism are final. Byte-splice, never re-encodes; disk file never touched (G3).

FOR nsy.4: iframe sandbox="allow-scripts" (no allow-same-origin); app-shell CSP in tauri.conf.json is still null — when you set it, allow frame-src artifact:. Use concrete version from artifact-updated payload (or /latest). Never point subresources at artifact:// (wry#168).

VERIFICATION: 16 unit tests on the factored core (parse incl. traversal/encoding, DB resolution incl. latest fallback, exact CSP string, stub placement/idempotence, pristine-disk check, styled 404s/400/405/500, HTML-escaping of reflected path input); tests save through the real artifacts::save_artifact so file naming is pinned in lockstep. cargo test --workspace 81 pass; just check clean. Live: app launched with isolated HOME — scheme registration doesn't disturb startup. NOT live-exercised: an actual WKWebView load of artifact:// and the fetch()-blocked-by-CSP behavior (needs the nsy.4 iframe to drive; CSP header value is exact per spec, enforcement is WebKit's).

title: Author the artifact specification doc (FR-3.x contract)
description: Write the formal contract between generating agents and the app (PRD §7.3) as an in-repo doc — it is later published inside the skill verbatim. Must specify: single self-contained .html (inline CSS/SVG/JS, no local file refs, network only from the §8 Tier-2 pinned-CDN allowlist with graceful degradation) (FR-3.1); WKWebView + browser dual-rendering constraint, Safari-compatible only (FR-3.2); anchorability — stable data-cfy-id on every section heading and meaningful diagram element (FR-3.3); diagram DSL sources embedded as HTML comments adjacent to rendered SVG (FR-3.4); metadata block cfy:question / cfy:version / cfy:generated-by in <head> (FR-3.5); and the validation rules save-artifact enforces (FR-3.6).
design: PRD §7.3, §8. This doc is the single source of truth — validator and skill must both cite it rather than restate it.
acceptance_criteria: Doc exists in-repo covering FR-3.1 through FR-3.6 precisely enough that (a) the save-artifact validator can be implemented from it and (b) the skill can embed it as authoring rules; Tier-2 allowlist enumerated with pinned versions.
notes:
Spec written at docs/artifact-spec.md. Decisions taken where PRD left latitude:
- data-cfy-id grammar: dot-separated kebab-case segments, regex ^[a-z](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?(?:\.[a-z](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?)*$, max 64 chars; semantic (never positional) naming mandated; diagram elements namespaced <figure-id>.<element>; deterministic derivation from DSL names (lowercase, -> becomes -to-, non-alnum runs collapse to -) so regeneration reproduces ids.
- Diagram source comment: <!--cfy:src lang=.. for=.. renderer=..-->, body escaped (\ then --> -> --\> , --!> -> --!\>) because Mermaid arrows (-->) would terminate an HTML comment; decode is single pass \\->\ , \>->>. Tier-2 runtime-rendered figures satisfy FR-3.4 via their mandatory <pre> fallback, no duplicate comment.
- Allowlist: single host cdn.jsdelivr.net (npm mirror) so CSP has one CDN host; prefix match with next-char-in {./} rule allows both @11 and @11.15.0 pins; charset [A-Za-z0-9@._/-] + no .. excludes query/fragment/escapes. highlight.js via @highlightjs/cdn-assets@11; markmap = markmap-lib/-view/-toolbar@0; ELK via @mermaid-js/layout-elk@0. Web fonts NOT allowlisted yet (deferred to M2 design pass / OQ1).
- Validation: rule IDs E-*/W-*. Hard: E-UTF8, E-PARSE (empty body), E-EXTERNAL-CODE (script src/link stylesheet|preload|modulepreload/@import outside allowlist), E-SIZE-MAX 50 MiB (added backstop beyond PRD letter). Warn: W-SIZE 5 MiB; thin coverage = any h1-h4 without id, or svg with >=6 shape elems and <3 anchored elems; plus doctype/charset/title/meta/version-mismatch/id-format/id-dup/src-malformed/src-orphan/external-ref/local-ref.
- cfy:version is informational; server-assigned version authoritative, mismatch = W-VERSION-MISMATCH.
- Reference runtime CSP documented in spec §3 (default-src none; script/style unsafe-inline + cdn.jsdelivr.net; font data:+cdn; img data:; connect none) — protocol handler must be at least this restrictive.

title: Tauri event wiring: live list updates
description: Close the live-update loop (PRD §5.3): the frontend subscribes to Tauri events emitted by the Rust core (thread-created, project changes; later artifact-updated, comment-resolved) and updates project/thread lists in place — no polling. This is the pattern all later live features (viewer refresh, sidebar answers) reuse, so establish clean event names and a single subscription layer.
acceptance_criteria: With the app visible, running 'conceptify create-thread' from a terminal makes the new thread appear in the list without any user interaction; event subscription is centralized (one module), not scattered per-component.
notes:
Implemented in src/lib/events.ts (single subscription module) + wired once from App.tsx useEffect (returns cleanup). Event->store mapping: projects-changed -> refetchProjects; thread-created -> refetchProjects + refetchThreads(payload.project_id); navigate -> refetchProjects, selectProject, then (if thread_id) AWAIT refetchThreads(project_id) BEFORE selectThread so a just-created target thread is in the list and the selection sticks (refetchThreads is a no-op unless its project is the selected one, which selectProject just made it). docs/api.md Events table verified accurate vs the Rust emit sites (payload field names/nullability match) - left untouched.

VERIFICATION GOTCHAS for downstream live-event beads (viewer-refresh, sidebar-answers):
- Tauri emit->JS listen was verified end-to-end via a temporary DB-observable proof (handler invoked rename_project with a sentinel derived from the event's thread_id; confirmed the isolated SQLite DB name changed to CFY_REACTED_<thread8>). Removed after.
- The proper harness is 'npm run tauri dev'. A directly-launched raw binary (./target/debug/conceptify-app with isolated HOME) started the server and webview but did NOT deliver emit->JS-listen events in that headless launch - use tauri dev to verify live events.
- Isolating HOME for app state breaks rustup ('no default toolchain') because it reads $HOME/.rustup; set RUSTUP_HOME=/Users/chrismeehan/.rustup and CARGO_HOME=/Users/chrismeehan/.cargo (real home) while keeping HOME=<tmp> so cargo works but the app writes state under the tmp HOME.
- Tauri v2 does NOT forward webview console.log to the dev terminal, and WKWebView console is not in os_log; a DB-observable temp invoke is the reliable external proof.

title: App shell UI: project sidebar + thread list + navigation
description: The navigation shell (PRD §5.3, FR-1.3, FR-2.2): project list sidebar (thread counts, last-activity, rename, archive; badge + re-map affordance for projects whose mapped directory no longer exists) → thread list per project (sorted by last activity, status chips generating/ready/updating/error, open-comment counts) → an empty thread-view placeholder that M2 fills with the artifact viewer. Keyboard-first navigation where cheap (⌘K switcher is Phase 2).
acceptance_criteria: Can navigate project → thread list → thread; rename and archive work from the UI; a project whose directory was deleted shows a badge and offers re-mapping; status chips render all four states.
notes:
Shell UI implemented (frontend in src/**, plus a small new src-tauri/src/commands.rs + additive registration in lib.rs). Do NOT close per orchestrator.

DATA-ACCESS DECISION (important for downstream): the frontend reaches the core via #[tauri::command] invoke, NOT the HTTP API. Reason: the webview origin (tauri://localhost) is cross-origin to 127.0.0.1:<port>, so every bearer-auth'd route triggers a CORS preflight the axum server doesn't answer (no CORS layer, which lives in the off-limits server/**); also only Rust can read the token/port files and stat the filesystem for the FR-1.3 'missing directory' badge. Commands added in src-tauri/src/commands.rs: list_projects, list_threads, rename_project, set_project_archived, remap_project. They wrap the existing projects/threads domain fns; rename_all=snake_case so JS invoke keys match Rust params. list_projects DTO adds root_exists (command-only enrichment; the HTTP ProjectListItem lacks it).

RE-MAP: no HTTP/domain endpoint existed for updating root_path. Added remap_project as a command with the canonicalize+UPDATE inlined in commands.rs (NOT added to projects.rs — the parallel agent was actively editing projects.rs, so I avoided it to prevent conflicts). UI uses a text-path input; when bead 959.3 lands the dialog plugin, swap it for a native dir picker.

FOR qxr.5 (event wiring): central store is src/store/appStore.ts (module-level observable + useAppStore hook). Seams: appStore.refetchProjects() and appStore.refetchThreads(projectId). Wire listen('projects-changed')->refetchProjects and listen('thread-created', e)->{refetchProjects(); refetchThreads(e.payload.project_id)} in a top-level effect (example in appStore.ts header comment). My command mutations do NOT emit Tauri events; the store refetches after its own mutations, so qxr.5 only needs to cover CLI/skill-originated events. refetchThreads(id) is a no-op unless id is the selected project, and both refetches guard against stale/out-of-order results.

Gates: npm run build, just check (clippy -D warnings), cargo test --workspace all pass. Added an IPC-level test (commands::tests) driving invoke('list_projects',{include_archived}) through Tauri dispatch to prove arg-mapping + root_exists. GUI not driven headlessly (WKWebView).

title: CLI: ensure-project, create-thread, open
description: Grow the CLI command surface (PRD §5.2) over the M1 endpoints: 'conceptify ensure-project --dir <path> [--name <name>]' → {projectId, created}; 'conceptify create-thread --project <id> --title <t> --question <q>' → {threadId}; 'conceptify open --thread <id> | --project <id>' → focuses the app on the target (bring window to front, navigate). JSON on stdout — these are the exact commands the M3 skill will drive.
design: PRD §5.2 command table. Focus-on-open is part of UC1's feel: the artifact should be on screen when the agent finishes.
acceptance_criteria: Each command works against a running app and via the cold-launch path; output is stable parseable JSON; open brings the app window to the foreground showing the right project/thread.
notes:
Implemented ensure-project, create-thread, open (qxr.3).

New endpoint: POST /api/v1/open (authed). Body: exactly one of {thread_id} or {project_id}; thread_id wins if both. Validates target -> 404 (thread/project not found), 400 if neither. On success: brings 'main' window to front (show()+set_focus() — window HIDES on close so must show first) and emits Tauri event 'navigate' with {project_id, thread_id|null}. Response {ok, project_id, thread_id}. Documented in docs/api.md.

FRONTEND (qxr.4/qxr.5): subscribe to 'navigate' event {project_id, thread_id: string|null} to route to the target. thread_id null => open project view, no specific thread.

CLI output contract is camelCase (projectId, threadId) even though the HTTP API is snake_case — the CLI is the stable contract the M3 skill parses. create-thread output includes 'slug' (needed for save-artifact artifact folder). ensure-project output is exactly {projectId, created} per PRD table.

Key decision: CLI canonicalizes --dir to an absolute path BEFORE sending, because the server canonicalizes relative to ITS cwd (wherever the app launched), not the agent's cwd — a bare relative --dir would resolve wrong. Nonexistent --dir fails fast on the CLI.

Kept deps lean: hand-rolled --key value flag parser (no clap), matching the existing skeleton's style. authed_post() helper reads token file, sends Bearer, surfaces server {error} messages verbatim on stderr with exit 1.

Verified end-to-end against a live debug app binary (isolated HOME so the real user DB was untouched): all 3 commands return correct JSON; idempotent ensure-project (created:true then false, same id); slug dedupe (-2); open --thread resolves to its project; open --project => threadId null; server logs showed no 'no main window'/emit-failure lines (focus+navigate emit ran clean). Raw curl confirmed /open: 401 no-auth, 400 no-target, 404 unknown, 200 valid. Cold-launch path (open -a Conceptify) unchanged skeleton code — not re-driven since it would launch the stale release bundle, not this new code. 27 tests pass; clippy -D warnings clean.

title: Threads backend: create + list (API)
description: Thread lifecycle basics (FR-2.1, FR-2.2 backend): create a thread (project_id, title, initial_question; slug generated from title for the artifact folder; status starts 'generating' per OQ4's current spec — create early, error status + retry on failure) and list threads per project sorted by last activity with status + open-comment counts. Mutations emit Tauri events (thread-created etc.).
design: PRD §7.2, §4 Thread entity. Status machine: generating | ready | updating | error — transitions owned by save-artifact and run lifecycle in later milestones.
acceptance_criteria: Thread create returns id + slug; slugs are filesystem-safe and unique within a project; list is sorted by last activity and includes status and open-comment count; thread-created event fires.
notes:
IMPLEMENTATION (qxr.2 threads backend create+list):

- SLUG PERSISTED AS A COLUMN, not derived on the fly. Added via APPENDED migration #7 (THREAD_SLUG) in db/migrations.rs, NOT by editing the shipped THREADS constant (append-only rule in that file). Verified: real dev DB was at user_version=6 with no slug column; editing THREADS in place would have broken it. Migration = 'ALTER TABLE threads ADD COLUMN slug TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''; CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_threads_project_slug ON threads(project_id, slug)'. SQLite can't add a UNIQUE column inline, hence the separate composite unique index. DEFAULT '' only affects pre-existing rows (there are none). Verified the 6->7 upgrade path non-destructively against a copy of the real dev DB.

- Slug is the STABLE artifact-folder name (§5.6 <thread-slug>), so it MUST stay put if the thread title is later renamed — downstream rename/save-artifact beads must NOT regenerate the slug from the (new) title. Persisted precisely so it's stable.

- slugify(): ASCII-only, dependency-free (no transliteration crate). Lowercases alphanumerics, collapses every other char (whitespace/punct/path-seps/non-ASCII) to single hyphens, no leading/trailing hyphen, capped at 80 chars. Titles that reduce to empty (emoji/CJK/punctuation-only) fall back to 'thread'. Per-project uniqueness via dedupe_slug (slug, slug-2, slug-3...), mirroring projects::dedupe_name.

- open_comment_count is a REAL LEFT JOIN on the existing comments table (COUNT WHERE status='open'), not a literal 0 — stays correct once comments backend (94m.2) inserts rows.

- ThreadStatus enum (generating|ready|updating|error) defined in threads.rs. Only Generating produced on create; other variants constructed by from_db_str() on read (keeps all 4 live under clippy -D warnings). Transitions past 'generating' owned by later beads.

- EVENT: POST /threads emits 'thread-created' with payload { project_id, thread_id } (NOT null like projects-changed) so qxr.5 can refresh just the affected project's list. Documented in docs/api.md.

- ROUTES (flat, mirroring projects): POST /api/v1/threads (project_id/title/initial_question in body), GET /api/v1/threads?project_id=<id>. Unknown project_id -> list returns empty array (no 404), matching list_projects; create -> 404 ProjectNotFound, 400 EmptyTitle.

FILES: crates/conceptify-types/src/lib.rs (CreateThread{Request,Response}, ThreadListItem, ListThreadsResponse); src-tauri/src/threads.rs (new domain module + unit tests); src-tauri/src/db/migrations.rs (THREAD_SLUG); src-tauri/src/server/threads_routes.rs (new); server/mod.rs + routes.rs (wiring); lib.rs (mod threads + real-migration integration test); docs/api.md.

NOT closed/committed per orchestrator instruction. just check + cargo test (13 passed) green.

title: Projects backend: ensure-project, create, list, rename, archive (API)
description: Project CRUD in the Rust core (FR-1.1, FR-1.3 backend): ensure-project by directory — given a root path, canonicalize it and return the existing project or create one, defaulting name to the directory name (deduped with a numeric suffix if taken); one project per path (unique root_path). Plus list (with thread counts + last-activity), rename, and archive (hide, don't delete). Expose under /api/v1/. Mutations emit Tauri events for live UI (§7.8).
design: PRD §7.1, §4 Project entity. Canonicalization is the identity rule — symlinks/trailing slashes must not create duplicate projects.
acceptance_criteria: POST ensure-project twice with the same dir (including a symlinked variant of the path) returns the same project with created:false the second time; name collisions dedupe with suffix; archive hides from default list but doesn't delete; list returns thread counts and last-activity; mutations emit events.
