title: OQ2: artifact storage location — in-repo .conceptify/ vs centralized
description: Open question 2 (PRD §12): where do artifact files live? Option A (current §5.6 assumption): <project_root>/.conceptify/threads/<slug>/artifact.vN.html — travels with the repo, human-visible, but pollutes it (needs global-gitignore suggestion; skill must never commit it). Option B: centralized ~/Documents/conceptify/artifacts/<project>/ — clean repos, less portable. PRD calls this 'cheap to flip in M0 — decide before M2' because the save-artifact storage layout (M2) bakes it in. Decide and record the rationale here; update §5.6 if flipping.
acceptance_criteria: A decision is recorded (with rationale) and the save-artifact storage task is unblocked with a concrete path scheme; prd.md updated if the §5.6 assumption changes.
notes:
DECISION: Option B — centralized artifact storage, keyed by project id, NOT in-repo.

RATIONALE:
1. The stated benefit of Option A ("travels with the repo") is negated by its own constraint: .conceptify/ must never be committed, so it can never actually travel via the mechanism repos travel by (clone/push/pull). It would only travel via non-git copies (rsync/tar), which is not how this codebase's users move repos around.
2. Concrete data-loss risk unique to in-repo storage: any routine git-hygiene command a developer runs inside the repo — `git clean -fdx`, `git clean -fd`, careless `rm -rf` on a "throwaway" clone — silently deletes an untracked .conceptify/ directory with zero recourse, while the SQLite metadata DB (in Application Support, untouched) still references now-missing files. That's a split-brain state directly contrary to N4's "never corrupt state" spirit, and the app has no way to intercept it since it's a plain filesystem/git operation outside Conceptify's control.
3. Repo directories are more volatile than the artifacts they'd host: users delete/re-clone/rename/relocate working directories far more casually than they'd want to lose saved Q&A + comment history. FR-1.3 already anticipates root_path going missing (badge + re-map affordance) — that volatility is a known hazard, not a hypothetical. Centralizing decouples artifact lifetime from root_path entirely: moving/renaming/deleting the mapped directory only requires repairing the root_path mapping, never touches artifact files.
4. Removes real engineering/UX surface: no "suggest global gitignore" onboarding step, no defensive "skill must never commit this" hazard, no risk of an errant `git add -A`/`git add .` scooping multi-MB HTML artifacts into a commit/PR history permanently.
5. Simpler for M2 (conceptify-nsy.3): one storage root, one code path, independent of whether a project is a mapped repo or an auto-created topic folder (auto-created projects already lived under ~/Documents/conceptify/projects/<name>/ as their root_path — this decision just stops conflating "the project's working directory" with "where Conceptify's own artifact files live").
6. Cost accepted: less casually human-visible in Finder next to the code, and nominally "less portable" if someone expected to hand off a repo folder with artifacts baked in — both mitigated by existing/planned features: FR-2.5 "Open in browser" is the per-artifact portability guarantee, and Phase-2 already plans export/share bundling for deliberate hand-off.

PATH SCHEME (concrete, replaces §5.6 in-repo layout):

~/Documents/conceptify/
  artifacts/
    <project-id>/                 (opaque DB primary key; immutable across rename/re-map, FR-1.3)
      threads/
        <thread-slug>/
          artifact.v1.html
          artifact.v2.html        <- follow-up updates append versions, monotonically increasing per thread
          artifact.html           <- atomic COPY (not symlink) of the latest version; written via temp+rename same as versioned files (N4), so it can never dangle/go stale
          runs/<run-id>.log       <- headless agent transcripts
  projects/
    <slugified-name>/             (unchanged — root_path for auto-created/non-codebase projects only; a working directory for agents, orthogonal to artifact storage above)

Thread-slug is generated once at thread creation (FR-2.1) and never renamed, so its directory never churns. Project-id (not project name) is the storage key so renaming a project (FR-1.3) never requires moving files on disk. save-artifact's "validates, stores" semantics (§5.2) mean the agent can author its draft HTML anywhere (its own cwd is fine) and hand a --file path to the CLI; the Rust core copies/moves it into this canonical location — so nothing about the skill's authoring flow (FR-6.1) depended on in-repo storage in the first place.

PRD EDITS: §5.6 storage layout block and prose rewritten to the centralized scheme above (removed the global-gitignore footnote, since it's no longer needed); §12 OQ2 marked RESOLVED with a one-paragraph pointer back to this bead.

UNBLOCKS: conceptify-nsy.3 (save-artifact endpoint) can now implement against this concrete layout.
close_reason: Decided Option B: centralized ~/Documents/conceptify/artifacts/<project-id>/ storage; rationale in notes and prd.md §12

title: conceptify CLI skeleton: shared workspace + launch-and-wait contract
description: CLI foundation (PRD §5.2): a small Rust binary in the same Cargo workspace as the app (shared request/response types with the server — no Node dependency, instant start). Implement the liveness/launch contract every command uses: probe GET /health (reading the port file, falling back to 4477), on failure run 'open -a Conceptify', poll /health up to ~10s, then proceed. Read the bearer token from the token file. Implement 'conceptify status' (app/API health, version) as the first real command; the command surface grows in later milestones. JSON output on stdout for agent consumption, human-readable errors on stderr.
design: PRD §5.2. This contract deliberately sidesteps macOS single-instance argv-forwarding quirks (plugins-workspace #1613) — 'launch and focus' is just HTTP.
acceptance_criteria: With the app stopped, 'conceptify status' launches the app, waits for /health, and prints JSON health info; with the app running, it responds in <50ms (N3); exits non-zero with a clear message if the app can't be reached within the timeout.
notes: Orchestrator addendum: the app crate and CLI both produced a binary named 'conceptify' in the shared workspace target/ (cargo output filename collision, last-built-wins). Fixed by renaming the app binary to 'conceptify-app' ([[bin]] in src-tauri/Cargo.toml + mainBinaryName in tauri.conf.json); the bundler still ships Conceptify.app via productName. CLI owns the 'conceptify' name. E2E verified: with app running, 'conceptify status' returns correct JSON in <10ms warm (first-ever run ~1s due to macOS Gatekeeper verification of a freshly built binary, not CLI logic).
close_reason: CLI skeleton complete: workspace (src-tauri + conceptify-types + conceptify-cli), launch-and-wait contract, 'conceptify status' verified E2E <10ms; app binary renamed conceptify-app to resolve target/ collision

title: SQLite layer: rusqlite + migrations + §4 schema
description: Database foundation (PRD §5.1, §5.6): rusqlite (bundled SQLite, WAL mode) held in Tauri managed state so both #[tauri::command] handlers and axum handlers share it. Explicitly NOT tauri-plugin-sql (no first-class Rust-side query API — tauri discussion #11527). Migrations via rusqlite_migration or a user_version scheme. Implement the §4 domain model schema: projects (id, name, root_path UNIQUE, created_at, archived), threads (id, project_id, title, initial_question, status generating|ready|updating|error, created_at, updated_at), artifacts (id, thread_id, version, file_path, created_at, created_by initial|follow_up), comments (id, thread_id, artifact_version, anchor JSON, body, status open|answered|applied, answer_html, created_at, resolved_at), follow_up_runs (id, thread_id, agent, model, mode answer|apply, status, log_path, started_at, finished_at), settings. DB stores artifact paths, never HTML blobs.
design: PRD §4, §5.6 rationale: metadata needs transactional integrity → SQLite; artifacts stay as plain files on disk.
acceptance_criteria: DB created at ~/Library/Application Support/conceptify/conceptify.db in WAL mode; migrations run idempotently on startup; all six §4 entities have tables with the specified fields and constraints (unique root_path); both a tauri command and an axum handler can query through managed state.
notes:
Implementation notes (bd conceptify-36s.3):

Module layout: src-tauri/src/db/{mod,migrations}.rs. `db::DbHandle` =
Arc<Mutex<rusqlite::Connection>>, opened once in db::init() (called from
lib.rs's setup hook, before server::start is spawned) and put into
app.manage(db). server::start() pulls it back out via
app_handle.state::<db::DbHandle>().inner().clone() and adds it as a `db`
field on ApiState (server/state.rs), so axum handlers reach it the same way
Tauri commands do. db::with_conn() wraps axum-side access in
tokio::task::spawn_blocking so a query never blocks an axum worker thread
while holding the Mutex; #[tauri::command] handlers lock DbHandle directly
(Tauri already runs command bodies off the async reactor thread).

Deps added to src-tauri/Cargo.toml: rusqlite 0.40 (bundled feature -
confirmed via Context7/cargo info as the current release; PRD Appendix A
cites 0.37, superseded), rusqlite_migration 2.6. Migrations are 6 M::up()
entries (one per §4 entity) in db/migrations.rs, run via
Migrations::to_latest() against user_version - idempotent by construction
(a second run against an already-migrated DB just reads one pragma, no DDL
re-executes). journal_mode=WAL and foreign_keys=ON are set once in
db::init() *before* to_latest() runs, per rusqlite_migration's own docs
("PRAGMA statements are generally discouraged" inside M::up).

Schema decisions worth flagging for conceptify-qxr.1 (projects backend) and
sibling CRUD beads:
- All `id` columns are TEXT PRIMARY KEY with no generation scheme baked in
  here - deliberately left to the CRUD beads (no uuid crate was added; add
  whatever you need, e.g. `uuid` v4, when you write the first INSERT).
- CHECK constraints added exactly where PRD §4 enumerates values in parens:
  threads.status, comments.status, artifacts.created_by, and
  follow_up_runs.mode. follow_up_runs.status has NO CHECK constraint - PRD
  §4's table lists it as plain "status" without an enumerated value set
  (unlike threads.status), so it's free-form TEXT; conceptify-b12.1 (agent
  spawner) should define/document its actual value set (candidates per
  FR-4.8: running/completed/failed/cancelled) when it starts writing rows.
- comments has a COMPOSITE foreign key: FOREIGN KEY (thread_id,
  artifact_version) REFERENCES artifacts(thread_id, version) ON DELETE
  CASCADE. This enforces that a comment can only be inserted against an
  artifact version that already exists in the artifacts table - insert the
  artifacts row for a version before inserting comments anchored to it.
  Verified this fires correctly (manual sqlite3 test, see below).
- All parent-child relations use ON DELETE CASCADE (project -> threads ->
  artifacts/comments/follow_up_runs), so deleting a project cascades
  everything under it. Nothing currently exercises delete paths - just be
  aware cascade is live once foreign_keys=ON (which db::init() sets on the
  shared connection every launch).
- Timestamps are TEXT, ISO-8601 UTC via strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%fZ',
  'now') (sortable, includes fractional seconds), not SQLite's
  CURRENT_TIMESTAMP.
- settings is a plain key/value TEXT table (key PK, value TEXT), not fixed
  columns - PRD §4 lists settings as free-form examples, not a schema.

New route: GET /api/v1/debug/db-check (authenticated) - documented in
docs/api.md. Demo #[tauri::command] db_check in lib.rs mirrors it for the
frontend side. Added a Rust-side IPC test (src-tauri/src/lib.rs
tests::db_check_command_reads_through_managed_state, using
tauri::test::mock_builder/get_ipc_response, gated by a `tauri` dev-dependency
with the "test" feature) since there's no headless way to poke the real
WKWebView devtools in this environment (same limitation noted on bead
conceptify-36s.2) - this exercises the actual IPC dispatch path against a
throwaway temp-file DB, not just a compile-time check. `cargo test` passes.

Verification performed (all against a real running instance - see below for
why no separate npm run tauri dev session was used for the HTTP parts):
1. DB file exists at ~/Library/Application Support/conceptify/conceptify.db;
   `sqlite3 ... "PRAGMA journal_mode;"` -> wal. Confirmed (plus
   conceptify.db-wal/-shm sidecars present).
2. Idempotent relaunch: confirmed two ways - (a) a second, independent
   process (my own `npm run tauri dev` launch) opened the same DB file and
   ran db::init() again with no errors before its server logic deferred to
   an already-running instance (proves setup() succeeded a second time
   against an already-migrated DB); (b) `sqlite3 ... "PRAGMA user_version;"`
   reads 6, matching the 6 defined migrations, stable across runs.
3. All six tables present with correct columns via `sqlite3 ... ".schema"`.
   Constraint enforcement verified directly with transactional (rolled
   back, non-destructive) INSERTs: root_path UNIQUE fires ("UNIQUE
   constraint failed: projects.root_path"), threads.status CHECK fires on
   an invalid value, and the FK on threads.project_id fires
   ("FOREIGN KEY constraint failed") with foreign_keys=ON. No rows leaked
   (verified count(*) = 0 afterward).
4. Both paths verified live: `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $(cat
   .../token)" http://127.0.0.1:<port>/api/v1/debug/db-check` ->
   {"ok":true,"project_count":0}; and the `db_check` command was invoked
   via tauri::test's real IPC dispatch in an automated test (see above),
   returning 0.

Environment note: while working this bead, a concurrent agent (CLI-skeleton
work) was actively running its own test cycles against a live Conceptify.app
instance built from this same working tree (same repo, no isolation) - that
instance ended up being the one used for steps 1/3/4's live curl check,
since my own dev launch correctly deferred to it (port-fallback/occupant-
detection working as designed). I did not kill that instance; I only killed
processes I personally started (my own `npm run tauri dev` + child
cargo-run'd app + vite, confirmed via ps/lsof afterward - no stray listeners
on 4477-4487 or dev servers left running that I started). Also confirmed:
the repo now has a root Cargo.toml (workspace: src-tauri +
crates/conceptify-types + crates/conceptify-cli), created by that same
concurrent agent - all work here stayed inside src-tauri/ + docs/api.md per
the task boundary; `cargo check`/`cargo test`/`cargo clippy --all-targets`
all run clean from within src-tauri using the new workspace target/.
close_reason: rusqlite + WAL + 6 idempotent migrations implementing the full §4 schema with CHECK/FK constraints; DbHandle shared via Tauri managed state to both tauri commands and axum handlers; verified live + via cargo test

title: axum HTTP API server in Tauri setup hook (health, token, port files)
description: Embed the local HTTP API (PRD §5.1): axum 0.8 spawned in the Tauri setup hook via tauri::async_runtime::spawn, bound to 127.0.0.1:4477. Share the AppHandle into axum state so handlers can emit Tauri events. Auth: random bearer token generated on first run, written mode 0600 to ~/Library/Application Support/conceptify/token; all endpoints except GET /health require it (S1). Port conflicts: on AddrInUse probe /health — if another Conceptify answers, defer to it; otherwise fall back through 4477-4487 and write the bound port to .../conceptify/port for CLI discovery. Routes live under /api/v1/ from day one (FR-8/§7.8).
design: PRD §5.1, §7.8, §9 S1. Versioned API path from day one; keep a single OpenAPI-ish reference doc in-repo shared by CLI, skill, and app.
acceptance_criteria: GET /health responds unauthenticated; any other route 401s without the bearer token; token file exists with 0600 perms; occupying 4477 with another process makes the app bind 4478 and update the port file; an axum handler can emit a Tauri event received by the webview.
notes:
Implementation notes (verified manually, see session transcript for full curl/log output):

Module layout: src-tauri/src/server/{mod,state,paths,auth,net,routes}.rs, wired
from lib.rs via `.setup(|app| { tauri::async_runtime::spawn(server::start(app.handle().clone())); Ok(()) })`.
- state.rs: ApiState { app_handle: AppHandle, token: Arc<str> } (Clone, used as axum State).
- paths.rs: ~/Library/Application Support/conceptify/{token,port} via dirs::data_dir()
  (deliberately NOT app.path().app_data_dir(), which would nest under the
  com.chrismeehan.conceptify bundle id — CLI needs a fixed path with no Tauri context).
- auth.rs: 32 random bytes (rand 0.9, rand::rng()) hex-encoded, temp+rename write,
  chmod 0600 (also re-asserted on reuse). Reused across restarts (loaded if present).
  require_bearer_token is an axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state layer.
- net.rs: bind_with_fallback() loops 4477..=4487; on AddrInUse probes the occupant's
  /health with a raw TCP GET + 750ms timeout, looking for the substring
  "\"service\":\"conceptify\"" in the body to distinguish a real Conceptify instance
  from an unrelated process. Match -> BindOutcome::DeferToExisting (logs, does not
  bind anywhere, does not touch the port file). No match -> tries next port. All
  ports exhausted -> BindOutcome::NoPortAvailable (logs, server simply doesn't start;
  never panics/crashes per N4).
- routes.rs: root GET /health + /api/v1/health both unauthenticated (mirrors both
  shapes mentioned in the bead text); /api/v1/ping is the demo authenticated route
  AND doubles as the event-emission demo (emits "api-ping" via AppHandle::emit,
  logged server-side). Frontend listener added in src/App.tsx (useEffect + listen()
  from @tauri-apps/api/event) logging to console.
- docs/api.md: auth scheme, discovery/port-fallback behavior, versioning rule,
  events table, /health and /api/v1/ping reference. Extend this doc, don't fork a
  second one, as later beads add routes.

Verification performed (npm run tauri dev, then killed; also ran the built
target/debug/conceptify binary directly for the two-instance defer test):
1. curl /health and /api/v1/health -> 200 unauthenticated. Confirmed.
2. curl /api/v1/ping without token -> 401; with token from token file -> 200
   {"pong":true}; with wrong token -> 401. Confirmed.
3. stat -f "%Lp" token -> 600. Confirmed.
4. Occupied 4477 with `python3 -m http.server`, relaunched app -> logs show
   "port 4477 occupied by an unrelated process; trying next port" ->
   "listening on 127.0.0.1:4478"; port file contained "4478";
   curl 127.0.0.1:4478/health -> 200. Confirmed.
5. Additionally verified the DeferToExisting branch directly: ran the compiled
   binary twice back-to-back (A binds 4477; B hits AddrInUse, probes, sees
   "service":"conceptify" in A's /health body, logs
   "deferring to it" and does NOT bind 4478); confirmed via lsof only one
   listener existed and the port file still read 4477.
   Event emission: server-side log confirms app_handle.emit("api-ping", ...)
   returns Ok on every /api/v1/ping call. Frontend listener code added,
   type-checks and the vite/tsc build passes. Could not visually confirm the
   console.log firing inside WKWebView's inspector in this headless run (no
   GUI/devtools automation tool available in this environment) - this is the
   one criterion only partially confirmed end-to-end; recommend a quick manual
   check (Safari > Develop > open the running app's inspector, hit
   /api/v1/ping, watch the console) before fully trusting the wire-up, though
   the code path is standard `@tauri-apps/api/event` usage and the emit side
   is proven to execute without error.

All test processes (python http.server, direct binary launches, npm run tauri
dev) were killed after verification; ports 4477-4487 confirmed free afterward.

Gotchas for downstream beads:
- 36s.3 (SQLite): rusqlite connection should go into Tauri managed state
  (`app.manage(...)`) in the same setup hook, then be added as a field on
  ApiState (or a second State extractor) so axum handlers can reach it -
  ApiState is intentionally a plain struct, not wrapped in Arc itself, so
  adding fields is a one-line change; just remember DB access from axum
  handlers needs a Mutex/pool since ApiState is cloned per request.
- 36s.5 (CLI): read the token from ~/Library/Application Support/conceptify/token
  and the port from .../port (plain integer, no newline handling needed but
  trim anyway defensively). The CLI's liveness probe should hit GET /health
  (unauthenticated, no token needed) at the discovered port before doing
  anything else, per the launch-and-wait contract in PRD §5.2. Note port file
  is NOT deleted/reset between runs - if the app is not currently running, the
  file may contain a stale port from a previous session; the CLI must still
  verify liveness via /health rather than trusting the file blindly.
- Both: docs/api.md is the shared reference - add sections there rather than
  writing new docs.
close_reason: axum 0.8 API embedded in Tauri setup hook: /health unauthenticated, bearer-token auth (0600 token file), port fallback 4477-4487 with defer-to-existing-instance, port file, event emission wired; all acceptance criteria verified

title: M4 — Comments (annotation layer)
description: Milestone 4 (PRD §11 M4): the first half of the crown-jewel interrogation loop (G5, §7.4). Covers the bridge script + server-side injection, text-selection comments with W3C-style anchors, diagram-element comments via data-cfy-id, the direct follow-up composer, the comments sidebar (filters, statuses, scroll-to-anchor, persistent highlights), and anchor re-attachment across artifact versions with 'reference moved' handling. Demoable outcome: highlight text or click a diagram node, leave a comment, see it anchored and highlighted.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed; text and diagram comments can be created, listed, filtered, and clicked-to-scroll; anchors survive artifact version switches or degrade to 'reference moved' — never silently dropped.

title: M3 — Skill v1: agent → app end-to-end (authoring integration)
description: Milestone 3 (PRD §11 M3): the Claude Code skill (§7.6) makes 'use conceptify to explain X' work from any repo. Covers the skill flow (FR-6.1), authoring guide with design scaffold + tiered visual strategy and exact render commands (FR-6.2, §8), the self-review loop (FR-6.3), conceptify doctor (FR-6.5), and quality iteration on real repos. Checkpoint: UC1/UC2/UC3 work start-to-finish from a cold app.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed; M3 checkpoint task passes: UC1 (explain from session), UC2 (syntax question), UC3 (codebase tour) all work from a cold app with consistently good artifact quality.

title: M2 — Artifact pipeline & reading experience (the viewer)
description: Milestone 2 (PRD §11 M2): artifacts flow in and render beautifully. Covers the artifact:// protocol handler with per-response CSP, the sandboxed-iframe viewer, the save-artifact endpoint (validation, versioning, atomic writes, live refresh), the design-system CSS scaffold (D1-D6), the artifact spec doc (§7.3), version switcher, and open-in-browser. Checkpoint: a hand-written test artifact renders beautifully in-app AND in Safari/Chrome — the portability guarantee (G3).
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed; M2 checkpoint task passes: hand-written test artifact renders correctly in-app and standalone in Safari and Chrome.

title: M1 — Projects & threads (core data + shell UI)
description: Milestone 1 (PRD §11 M1): the core domain objects become real. Projects (ensure/create/list/rename/archive) and threads (create/list) exposed through API + CLI + UI, app shell navigation (project sidebar → thread list), and Tauri event wiring so lists update live. Demoable outcome: create a project and thread from the CLI and watch them appear in the app without a refresh.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed; projects and threads manageable via API, CLI, and UI; list views update live via Tauri events.

title: M0 — Foundations (scaffolding)
description: Milestone 0 of Conceptify (PRD §11 M0): stand up the Tauri v2 app skeleton so every later milestone has rails to build on. Covers the app scaffold (Preact + Tailwind v4 + Vite), the axum HTTP API with token/port files, the rusqlite database with the §4 schema, app lifecycle behavior (single-instance, hide-on-close, window-state), the conceptify CLI skeleton with its launch-and-wait contract, and the justfile. Demoable outcome: app launches fast, /health answers, CLI can reach the API, DB schema migrated.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed; app cold-launches, GET /health responds, CLI probe-launch-poll contract works against a cold app, schema migrated in WAL-mode SQLite.
close_reason: All 7 children closed: app cold-launches with axum API + WAL SQLite, CLI probe-launch-poll contract verified E2E, lifecycle plugins wired, justfile entry points working

title: Inline <code> overflows horizontally at narrow pane widths (no overflow-wrap)
description: Found during the M2 checkpoint (nsy.7) end-to-end pass. At reading-pane widths below ~458px the artifact overflows horizontally (document.documentElement.scrollWidth 458 > clientWidth), producing a page-level horizontal scrollbar / cut-off content. Root cause: inline <code> in prose has overflow-wrap:normal (design-system.css line ~481, selector 'p code, li code, td code, th code, figcaption code, summary code, dd code'). A single unbreakable token in the demo — 'artifact://thread/<slug>/artifact.v3.html' (42 chars, inside a step <li>) — refuses to break and sets a fixed ~458px minimum page width. Everything else scales/clips correctly: <pre> blocks scroll internally (overflow-x:auto, verified rectW 240 / scrollW 549), tables use .cfy-table-wrap (overflow-x:auto), SVGs honor max-width:100% (both rendered at 190px in a 288px column). Where it bites in-app: the default 800px window minus sidebar (w-56=224) + thread list (w-72=288) leaves a 288px reading pane, so it shows on first run. Mitigated after one resize by tauri-plugin-window-state persistence (PRD line 105). At >=460px pane the artifact is flawless (verified gorgeous at 640/900px in Chrome + WebKit, light+dark).
design: Add 'overflow-wrap: break-word;' to the inline-code selector in skill/design-system.css (~line 481). break-word only breaks a token when it would otherwise overflow, so normal-width rendering is unchanged, and it is inert on 'pre code' (white-space:pre) so block-code horizontal scroll is unaffected. IMPORTANT: skill/examples/demo-artifact.html embeds the scaffold VERBATIM as its first <style> block, so make the identical edit there too (keep them byte-in-sync) and re-run 'just install-skill' to refresh the installed snapshot. Optional/related (product call, not required here): widen the initial window in src-tauri/tauri.conf.json (currently 800x600) so the first-run reading pane isn't 288px. Boundary note: nsy.7 left design-system.css untouched because it is a closed-bead (nsy.5) deliverable and a parallel agent was active; acceptance permits filing.
acceptance_criteria: At a 288px viewport the demo artifact has document.documentElement.scrollWidth == clientWidth (no horizontal page overflow); long inline-code tokens wrap; normal/wide-width rendering is visually unchanged; skill/design-system.css and the embedded copy in skill/examples/demo-artifact.html stay identical; installed skill re-snapshotted.
close_reason: Fixed: overflow-wrap:break-word on inline code in scaffold + demo embedded copy; skill reinstalled

title: Skill: consider a post-process helper script for generated SVG (id stamping + style stripping)
description: rendering.md currently instructs the authoring agent to post-process d2/dot SVG by hand (strip prolog/styles/ids, base64-decode d2 group classes, derive+stamp data-cfy-id per spec §4.4). The recipe is deterministic, so a bundled scripts/postprocess-svg.mjs (mirroring scripts/highlight.mjs) would cut per-artifact effort and eliminate derivation mistakes. Decide during 57l.4 quality iteration whether hand-editing error rates justify shipping it.

title: README + setup script
description: Make the repo self-installing (PRD §11 M6): README covering what Conceptify is, architecture sketch, prerequisites (d2, graphviz, node, claude), and setup; a setup script (driving the justfile) that builds the app, installs the CLI onto PATH, installs the skill to ~/.claude/skills/conceptify/, and suggests the .conceptify/ global-gitignore entry; points at conceptify doctor for verification.
acceptance_criteria: On a fresh clone: one documented command sequence yields a working app + CLI + skill; doctor passes; README accurate to what shipped.

title: Settings UI (FR-7.1 – FR-7.4)
description: Surface configuration (PRD §7.7): agent adapters — default adapter, command templates, per-purpose models (followUp/artifactUpdate/inAppAsk), timeout (FR-7.1); appearance light/dark/system (FR-7.2); paths — auto-project base dir, agent binary override (FR-7.3). Stored in SQLite via the settings model; sensible defaults mean the app works with ZERO configuration when claude is on PATH (FR-7.4) — settings UI is for overrides, not onboarding.
acceptance_criteria: Every §7.7 setting is editable and takes effect without restart where feasible; resetting to defaults works; invalid command templates rejected with a helpful message; fresh install runs correctly having never opened settings.

title: In-app project creation: dir picker + auto-create (FR-1.2)
description: UC6 (PRD FR-1.2): create a project from the app — native directory picker for existing dirs (repos), or 'create a folder for me' → ~/Documents/conceptify/projects/<slugified-name>/ for non-codebase topics (e.g. 'distributed systems'). Auto-create base dir configurable (FR-7.3). Reuses the ensure-project canonicalization path.
acceptance_criteria: Both flows create a usable project; auto-created folder exists at the configured base; picking an already-mapped directory lands on the existing project instead of erroring.

title: Error, timeout, and retry paths (FR-5.3)
description: Failure hygiene for in-app asks (PRD FR-5.3): agent crash or timeout (default 15 min, configurable) → thread status 'error', log viewable from the thread, one-click retry (re-spawns with the same question into the same thread). Applies the N4 rule: a failed run never corrupts thread state.
acceptance_criteria: Simulated crash and timeout both land the thread in 'error' with the log accessible; retry succeeds and the thread proceeds to ready; no orphaned processes or stuck 'generating' states.

title: OQ3: permission scoping for headless runs
description: Open question 3 (PRD §12): headless runs currently use --permission-mode acceptEdits — scoped how? Artifact-apply runs only need to write within the thread folder. Investigate per-adapter sandboxing/allowed-tools flags (Claude Code allowed-tools, permission modes; whatever Codex offers later) during M5 and pick the tightest workable scoping. Right-sized security (§9): containment and hygiene, not adversarial defense.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded; adapter default args updated to the chosen scoping; documented in the skill/README so future adapters follow the same principle.

title: OQ4: confirm early thread creation (generating status) feels right
description: Open question 4 (PRD §12): current spec creates the thread BEFORE the artifact exists (UC1 shows a 'generating' thread early — better UX) with error status + retry on agent failure, vs. only registering threads on save-artifact (no orphan threads). Confirm the chosen behavior feels right during M3 real-repo iteration; if orphaned error-threads become noise, revisit. Record the confirmation or the change.
acceptance_criteria: Decision confirmed or reversed based on M3 experience; thread-creation flow and skill updated if changed.

title: OQ5: is Mermaid pre-rendering worth its weight in the skill?
description: Open question 5 (PRD §12): decide empirically during M3 iteration whether Mermaid pre-rendering (mermaid-cli/Puppeteer toolchain) earns its place in the skill, or whether D2 + Graphviz DOT + hand-authored SVG covers all structured-diagram needs (§8 Tier 1). Weigh: toolchain weight and flakiness vs. cases where Mermaid's syntax reliability wins. Record the decision and update the skill's visual-strategy section accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded with evidence from M3 iterations; skill guide updated to match (either exact mermaid render commands, or explicit 'use D2/DOT instead' guidance).

title: conceptify doctor: prerequisites check (FR-6.5)
description: 'conceptify doctor' CLI command (PRD FR-6.5): checks app installed, CLI on PATH, d2 present, graphviz (dot) present, node present (for Shiki), agent binary resolvable (login-shell lookup per §5.1); prints actionable per-check pass/fail with install hints. Skill docs reference it as the first debugging step; prerequisites also documented in the repo.
acceptance_criteria: Doctor reports each dependency with clear pass/fail and remediation hint; exits non-zero when anything required is missing; documented in README/skill.

title: Skill self-review loop (FR-6.3)
description: De-risk the highest-variance tier — hand-authored SVG (PRD FR-6.3): before save-artifact, the skill instructs the agent to render the HTML (headless browser/screenshot) and review for overlapping labels, broken layout, and contrast issues, iterating until clean. Define the exact mechanism (which headless renderer, what the review checklist is) and bake it into the skill.
acceptance_criteria: Skill includes a working, copy-pasteable self-review procedure; demonstrated catching at least one real layout defect (e.g. overlapping SVG labels) before publish during M3 iteration.
notes:
FR-6.3 visual self-review loop (57l.2) — mechanism + verification:

MECHANISM: agent-browser (bundled headless Chromium, CDP) — the proven path from nsy.5/57l.1. Recipe: open file://<artifact>, then for each of 2 widths x 2 modes: 'set viewport <w> 900 2' + 'set media light|dark' + 'screenshot --full <png>', then Read the PNGs and judge against a checklist. Widths 460 (narrow thread-pane / overflow-risk) and 900 (comfortable); deviceScaleFactor 2 for crisp text. set viewport/set media apply live to the next screenshot — no reload needed (verified). Rejected alternatives: app-window screencapture (TCC-blocked, per prior beads), Playwright-via-npx (not installed here — confirmed unreliable; kept only as a best-effort fallback note).

MECHANICAL OVERFLOW TRIPWIRE (novel, verified): a '--full' PNG's pixelWidth == viewport_width x scale EXACTLY when there is no horizontal overflow (920 at 460/scale2; 1800 at 900/scale2). Anything wider = horizontal overflow at that width. 'sips -g pixelWidth' gives a cheap deterministic narrow-pane-overflow detector to pair with the visual read.

ANIMATION NOTE: headless Chromium runs animations to completion, so it CANNOT reproduce the WKWebView occlusion-suspension from-state trap — that stays a SOURCE check (scaffold ban, restated in checklist). Documented explicitly.

DELIVERABLES: new skill/references/self-review.md (recipe + mechanical check + 8-item checklist + loop + fallback); SKILL.md step 5 rewritten from placeholder to Source review + Visual review (points at self-review.md), and self-review.md added to the 'Files in this skill' list. install-skill picks it up automatically (cp -R skill/.) — no justfile change. Reinstalled; verified present in ~/.claude/skills/conceptify/.

VERIFIED E2E: golden fixture skill/examples/demo-artifact.html rendered clean on all 4 frames (widths match exactly: 920/920/1800/1800). Sabotaged scratchpad copy with 3 injected defects all caught: (1) overlapping SVG labels (moved fig-serve-path handler label x 350->150) visible in every frame; (2) hardcoded #211c15 fill on the hand-sketch 'opaque-origin iframe' label — readable in light, INVISIBLE in dark (dark-mode contrast break); (3) white-space:nowrap long command -> narrow-frame pixelWidth 1594 (>920) = horizontal overflow at 460px, exactly 1800 (clean) at 900px. Screenshots under scratchpad/57l2/.

title: justfile: dev / build / install-cli
description: Developer entry points (PRD §11 M0): a justfile with at minimum 'just dev' (app dev loop), 'just build' (release build of app + CLI), and 'just install-cli' (build the CLI and symlink it into /usr/local/bin or ~/.local/bin so it's on PATH for agents). Keep it the single place build/install knowledge lives — the M6 setup script and README will call into it.
acceptance_criteria: just dev / just build / just install-cli each work from a clean checkout; after install-cli, 'conceptify status' resolves from a fresh shell.
close_reason: justfile with dev/build/install-cli (+check) verified from clean state; install-cli symlinks to ~/.local/bin, fresh-shell 'conceptify status' works

title: App lifecycle: single-instance, hide-on-close, window-state
description: Lifecycle behavior (PRD §5.1): tauri-plugin-single-instance as duplicate-launch guard; window close HIDES instead of quitting (CloseRequested + prevent_close) so the HTTP API stays up while no window is visible; explicit Quit from the app menu; tauri-plugin-window-state for size/position persistence. This is what makes the CLI liveness contract cheap — the app being 'running' doesn't require a visible window.
design: PRD §5.1 Lifecycle. Menu-bar/tray mode is Phase 2 — plain hidden window is enough for Phase 1.
acceptance_criteria: Launching the app twice focuses the existing instance instead of starting a second; closing the window leaves the process alive and /health responding; reopening (e.g. via open -a) restores the window at its previous size/position; menu Quit actually exits.
close_reason: single-instance (registered first), hide-on-close via CloseRequested+prevent_close, macOS Reopen restore, window-state plugin; default menu Quit exits (ExitRequested not intercepted); verified live where headless allows
